commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0e4486b670a466fcc4fdbcb1c3db89a2e5d0dfb7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a manual `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` command with feed profile, feed URL, expected checksum, JSON output, URL redaction, credential-bearing URL rejection, and focused CLI/catalog tests.

PR surface: Source +204, Tests +322. Total +526 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR finding: a live fetch of the default ClawHub feed shows schemaVersion 2, while source inspection shows the loader accepts only schemaVersion 1. This is not a current-main bug because the refresh command is not present on main.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is the right lane because the PR is draft/protected and the live feed schema mismatch requires maintainer stack or producer direction plus contributor proof.

target.title: Add marketplace feed refresh command
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->
## Full hosted marketplace feed stack

We are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.

- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.
- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.
- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.
- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.
- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.
- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.
- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.

Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->

## Summary

Adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh`, a small manual activation slice for the hosted marketplace feed stack. The command reads `config.marketplaces`, calls the configured hosted external plugin catalog loader, and reports whether the accepted catalog came from the hosted feed, the last-known-good snapshot, or bundled fallback.

This gives Vince and maintainers a safe way to exercise the live `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins` path and any locally configured `marketplaces.feeds` profile without changing startup, onboarding, doctor, install, or provider-repair behavior yet.

## What changed

- Adds `plugins marketplace refresh` with:
  - `--feed-profile <name>` for a configured marketplace feed profile.
  - `--feed-url <url>` for the existing guarded explicit hosted URL path.
  - `--expected-sha256 <hash>` for checksum-pinned refresh checks.
  - `--json` for automation-friendly source/feed/metadata output.
- Keeps the command lazy-loaded with the existing plugin runtime boundary.
- Reports hosted, hosted-snapshot, and bundled-fallback results.
- Fails checksum-pinned refreshes when the accepted result is not a fresh hosted payload, so automation cannot silently accept bundled fallback after a checksum mismatch.
- Adds focused tests for CLI routing, refresh output, fallback reporting, and pinned-refresh failure.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base origin/feeds-hosted-catalog-config-profiles` initially found the pinned-refresh fallback exit issue; fixed in this PR and reran the same review clean: no actionable regressions found.
